concepts.title: Consumer Equilibrium
concepts.description: Consumer equilibrium occurs when the consumer maximizes the satisfaction or utility from the given budget. It is the point where the marginal utility of money (MUM) equals the price of the commodity.

concepts.title: Law of Demand
concepts.description: According to the law of demand, as the price of a commodity increases, the quantity demanded of it decreases, ceteris paribus.

concepts.title: Types of Consumer Equilibrium
concepts.description: There are two types of consumer equilibrium: Indifference Curve Equilibrium and Budget Line Equilibrium. Indifference Curve Equilibrium occurs when the consumer is indifferent between two bundles of goods, while Budget Line Equilibrium occurs when the consumer spends the entire budget.

concepts.title: Price Elasticity of Demand
concepts.description: To remember the formula for price elasticity of demand (PED = % change in quantity demanded / % change in price), use the mnemonic: 'Percentage of Price change, over Percentage of Quantity change'!

concepts.title: Confusing MUP with MRP
concepts.description: Students often confuse marginal utility per rupee (MUP) with marginal revenue product (MRP). MUP is the additional satisfaction obtained from one additional rupee spent, while MRP is the additional revenue obtained from one additional unit produced.

concepts.title: Law of Diminishing Marginal Utility (DMU)
concepts.description: The law of DMU states that as the consumption of a commodity increases, the marginal utility obtained from each additional unit decreases, ceteris paribus.

concepts.title: Indifference Curve
concepts.description: An indifference curve is a graphical representation of all the possible combinations of two goods that provide the same level of satisfaction or utility to the consumer.

concepts.title: Consumer's Budget
concepts.description: The consumer's budget is the total amount of money available for spending on all goods and services. It is the total quantity of all goods and services that the consumer can afford to buy.
